question: A six-vertex (6V) model is defined by arrow configurations on a kagome lattice that satisfy the two-in/two-out ice rule at every vertex, with vertex Boltzmann weights a, b, and c. The model exhibits partial vortex-lattice phases VL-H (honeycomb-face vortex lattice) and VL-T (triangular-face vortex lattice), which melt into a vortex-disordered phase VL-D. Monte Carlo simulations with finite-size scaling are used. What is the ORDER of the phase transition for the melting of the partial vortex-lattice phases VL-H and VL-T into the vortex-disordered phase VL-D? Answer in JSON with field "transition_order" using one of: "first_order", "continuous", "bkt". If the paper also identifies the universality class, include "universality_class" using one of: "2D Ising", "BKT", "3D XY", "3D Heisenberg", "chiral", "tricritical", "other".

metadata.system: Kagome-lattice six-vertex (6V) model, vortex-lattice phases VL-H and VL-T
metadata.source_excerpt: The melting transitions of the partial vortex-lattice phases VL-H and VL-T into the vortex-disordered phase VL-D behave as Berezinskii–Kosterlitz–Thouless (BKT) transitions in the numerical data accessed: inside the VL-H and VL-T phases measured vortex two-point correlation functions decay algebraically
metadata.difficulty_note: BKT identification requires algebraic decay and essential-singularity scaling; paper explicitly names BKT.

question: Two-flavor lattice QCD (N_f=2) is simulated with a one-loop Symanzik-improved gauge action and Asqtad staggered fermion action on lattices with temporal extent L_t=4 and spatial volumes L_s=12,16,20. The model exhibits Roberge-Weiss (RW) phase transitions at imaginary chemical potential mu=i*mu_I at theta=(2n+1)pi/3. Finite-size scaling of the Polyakov-loop susceptibility and Binder cumulant B_4 are used to classify the RW endpoint at bare quark mass am=0.038. What is the ORDER of the Roberge-Weiss transition endpoint at am=0.038? Answer in JSON with field "transition_order" using one of: "first_order", "continuous", "bkt". If the paper identifies the universality class, include "universality_class" using one of: "3D Ising", "tricritical", "mean-field", "other".

metadata.system: Lattice QCD N_f=2 with imaginary chemical potential, Roberge-Weiss endpoint at am=0.038
metadata.source_excerpt: at am=0.026, 0.038, 0.040 the finite-volume behavior and fitted exponents are consistent with a second-order RW endpoint in the three-dimensional Ising universality class
metadata.difficulty_note: Mass-dependent transition order; intermediate masses (0.026-0.040) are 3D Ising second-order while small/large masses are first-order triple points.

question: The pure Z_2 lattice gauge theory in four space-time dimensions (d=4) is simulated by Metropolis Monte Carlo with thermal cycles and quench experiments on 4^4 hypercubic lattices. The model has gauge coupling K and no matter (Higgs coupling h=0). The transition separates an electric confinement regime (small K) from an unconfined regime (large K). What is the ORDER of the bulk phase transition in the pure d=4 Z_2 lattice gauge theory? Answer in JSON with field "transition_order" using one of: "first_order", "continuous", "bkt".

metadata.system: Pure Z_2 lattice gauge theory on 4D hypercubic lattice, confinement-deconfinement transition
metadata.source_excerpt: The pure Z_2 lattice gauge theory on a four-dimensional hypercubic lattice with Higgs coupling set to h=0 exhibits a strongly first-order phase transition separating an electric confinement regime (Wilson loops obey an area law) for small gauge coupling K and an unconfined regime
metadata.difficulty_note: Clear strongly first-order result confirmed by hysteresis, metastability, and agreement with Creutz et al.

cold_solve.probe_model: claude-sonnet baseline (a cloud HPC platform sandbox for C1)

question: The compact three-dimensional U(1) lattice gauge theory (3D U(1) LGT) is studied on an asymmetric lattice L^2 x N_t at vanishing spatial coupling (beta_s=0). Spatial link variables are integrated out exactly, reducing the model to a two-dimensional effective spin model for Polyakov-loop angles. For N_t=1, the reduced model is the exact 2D XY model. Monte Carlo simulations on spatial lattices up to L=256 with finite-size scaling are used. The Svetitsky-Yaffe conjecture predicts 2D XY universality for this gauge theory. What is the ORDER of the finite-temperature deconfinement phase transition of the compact 3D U(1) lattice gauge theory at beta_s=0? Answer in JSON with field "transition_order" using one of: "first_order", "continuous", "bkt". Also include "universality_class" using one of: "2D XY", "BKT", "2D Ising", "3D XY", "other".

metadata.system: Compact 3D U(1) lattice gauge theory on L^2 x N_t lattice at beta_s=0, deconfinement transition
metadata.source_excerpt: The compact three-dimensional U(1) lattice gauge theory at finite temperature on a Lambda=L^2 x N_t lattice with vanishing spatial coupling beta_s=0... belongs to the 2D XY universality class (BKT transition), confirming the Svetitsky-Yaffe conjecture
metadata.difficulty_note: BKT confirmed by essential-singularity scaling of pseudo-critical coupling and eta=1/4 at criticality; N_t=8 has remaining ambiguity between BKT and first-order scaling.

question: An elastic Ising antiferromagnet is modeled as a binary alloy on a deformable diamond lattice with antiferromagnetic interactions described by the Stillinger-Weber potential. The system is studied by constant-pressure semigrand-canonical Monte Carlo simulations with system sizes up to linear dimension L=24 (N=8L^3 spins). The staggered composition (staggered magnetization) is the order parameter for a two-sublattice superstructure ordering transition. Field-theoretic predictions for this type of elastic coupling at constant pressure suggest a fluctuation-driven weak first-order transition, but prior simulations were inconclusive. What is the ORDER of the antiferromagnetic ordering phase transition found in these simulations? Answer in JSON with field "transition_order" using one of: "first_order", "continuous", "bkt". If the paper identifies the universality class, also include "universality_class" using one of: "3D Ising", "mean-field", "Fisher-renormalized", "other".

metadata.system: Binary alloy on deformable diamond lattice with Stillinger-Weber antiferromagnetic potential, constant pressure
metadata.source_excerpt: the phase transition along the observed phase boundary is consistent with a continuous second-order transition belonging to the universality class of the rigid-lattice three-dimensional Ising model
metadata.difficulty_note: Theory predicts weak first-order at constant pressure due to elastic coupling, but numerics at accessible sizes L<=24 show 3D Ising continuous behavior; weak first-order may emerge only at larger scales.

question: The compact U(1) gauge theory in four Euclidean dimensions is simulated using the Villain-equivalent world-sheet formulation, where integer-valued plaquette multiplicities n_p subject to the closed-surface constraint are updated by Metropolis on L^4 hypercubic lattices with periodic boundary conditions, for L from 6 to 24. The specific-heat maximum, Lee-Yang zeroes, energy histograms (latent heat), and autocorrelation times are analyzed. What is the ORDER of the confinement-to-Coulomb (deconfinement) phase transition of the compact U(1) gauge theory in this Villain formulation? Answer in JSON with field "transition_order" using one of: "first_order", "continuous", "bkt".

metadata.system: 4D compact U(1) gauge theory, Villain world-sheet formulation on L^4 hypercubic lattice
metadata.source_excerpt: Combining the independent finite-size analyses—specific-heat maxima scaling as L^4 for large L, nonzero extrapolated latent heat Delta e(infty) approx 0.012-0.014, a small finite interfacial free energy 2f_s ~ 1e-4, exponentially growing tunneling times... data are compatible with a very weak first-order phase transition
metadata.difficulty_note: Very weak first-order: latent heat ~0.012, interfacial free energy ~1e-4; Lee-Yang zeroes give nu=0.329(3) which interpolates between first-order (1/d=0.25) and continuous values.

question: The three-state Potts model on a three-dimensional simple cubic lattice is defined by H = -J1 * sum_{nn} delta(sigma_i, sigma_j) - J2 * sum_{nnn} delta(sigma_i, sigma_j), where lambda = J2/J1 is the ratio of next-to-nearest-neighbor to nearest-neighbor coupling. Prior smaller-lattice studies claimed a second-order (Z3 critical) transition for antiferromagnetic lambda=-0.2. Large-scale Monte Carlo simulations on 64^3 lattices with histogram analysis of the nearest-neighbor correlation C_nn and finite-volume peak-width scaling are used to characterize the transition at lambda=-0.2. What is the ORDER of the order-disorder transition of this 3D three-state Potts model at lambda=-0.2? Answer in JSON with field "transition_order" using one of: "first_order", "continuous", "bkt".

metadata.system: 3D three-state Potts model on simple cubic lattice with antiferromagnetic NNN coupling lambda=-0.2
metadata.source_excerpt: For the three-state Potts model on the three-dimensional simple cubic lattice... for the antiferromagnetic ratios lambda=-0.2 and lambda=-0.1, the order-disorder transition is a first-order phase transition. This determination is based on (a) long persistent tunneling between coexisting phases, (b) double-peaked histograms of C_nn, (c) peak widths narrowing as V^{-1/2}
metadata.difficulty_note: Prior work incorrectly claimed second-order Z3 criticality; large-volume simulation on 64^3 reveals weak but robust first-order with clear phase coexistence.

cold_solve.probe_model: claude-sonnet baseline (a cloud HPC platform sandbox for C1)

question: SU(N)/Z_N lattice gauge theories in four Euclidean dimensions are studied using Wilson's plaquette action taken in the adjoint representation (plaquette action S_square = 1 - (1/(N^2-1)) * Tr_A U_square). Monte Carlo simulations are performed on 4^4 lattices for N=3, 4, 5, 6, using mixed-start initial configurations. Observables include the average plaquette action and evidence of metastability, hysteresis, and superheating/supercooling. What is the ORDER of the finite-temperature phase transitions of these SU(N)/Z_N adjoint lattice gauge theories? Answer in JSON with field "transition_order" using one of: "first_order", "continuous", "bkt".

metadata.system: SU(N)/Z_N lattice gauge theories on 4^4 lattice with adjoint Wilson plaquette action, N=3,4,5,6
metadata.source_excerpt: For SU(N)/Z_N lattice gauge theories... N=3,4,5,6 exhibits a finite-temperature first-order phase transition... pronounced metastability and hysteresis: ordered and disordered starts converge to different long-lived values... clear examples of superheating and supercooling are observed
metadata.difficulty_note: First-order confirmed by metastability, hysteresis, superheating/supercooling on 4^4 lattices; removing the center Z_N does not eliminate first-order behavior.

question: The P^2 nematic lattice model has site variables hat{n}_r in P^2 (three-dimensional unit vectors with sign identification hat{n} <-> -hat{n}) and nearest-neighbor interaction Hamiltonian H = -J * sum_{<r,r'>} (1/2)[3(hat{n}_r . hat{n}_{r'})^2 - 1] with J<0 (favoring orthogonal nearest-neighbor orientations). Monte Carlo simulations are performed on three-dimensional cubic lattices. The J<0 model has a nonfrustrated, extensively degenerate ground state. The conventional J>0 nematic in d>=3 shows a first-order transition. What is the ORDER of the finite-temperature phase transition of the P^2 nematic model with J<0 on a three-dimensional cubic lattice? Answer in JSON with field "transition_order" using one of: "first_order", "continuous", "bkt".

metadata.system: P^2 nematic model with J<0 (orthogonally-favoring interaction) on 3D cubic lattice
metadata.source_excerpt: Monte Carlo simulations on three-dimensional cubic lattices give clear evidence of a finite-temperature phase transition... demonstrating a finite-temperature, symmetry-breaking, continuous transition with sublattice order
metadata.difficulty_note: Contrast with J>0 case which is first-order; J<0 is second-order due to symmetry of sublattice order parameter excluding cubic invariant in Landau expansion.

question: Pure gauge compact U(1) lattice gauge theory (compact lattice QED) is simulated on four-dimensional hypercubic lattices with FIXED boundary conditions: all link variables on the lattice boundary are fixed to the identity, eliminating monopole world-line loops that wrap around the periodic directions. Simulations are performed for lattice sizes L=6 to 16, measuring the per-plaquette internal energy, specific heat, and Binder cumulant. The energy Binder cumulant and specific-heat peak behavior are analyzed to determine the order of the confinement-deconfinement transition. What is the ORDER of the finite-temperature confinement-deconfinement phase transition of compact U(1) lattice gauge theory as inferred from these fixed-boundary simulations? Answer in JSON with field "transition_order" using one of: "first_order", "continuous", "bkt".

metadata.system: Compact U(1) lattice QED on 4D hypercubic lattice with fixed boundary conditions (identity links on boundary)
metadata.source_excerpt: Specific heat peak grows slowly and shifts toward beta~1, consistent with continuous transition... The combination of smooth energy variation under fixed-boundary conditions and the Binder cumulant tending to 2/3 imply periodic-boundary first-order signals are finite-volume topological artifacts
metadata.difficulty_note: Fixed-boundary (spherical topology) removes wrapped monopole artifacts that create spurious first-order signals in periodic simulations; result is continuous transition at ~beta=1.

question: The extended Z(4) lattice gauge model in four dimensions is defined by the two-parameter plaquette action S(J1,J2) = J1 * sum_P (1 - Re Tr U_P) + J2 * sum_P (1 - Re Tr U_P^2), where U_P is the ordered product of Z(4)-valued link variables around plaquette P. Heat-bath Monte Carlo simulations are performed on lattices from 4^4 to 8^4. The Z(4) phase diagram has four regions. The parameter region with J2>=0 (above or on the Wilson-action curve X2=X1^2 in the (X1,X2) Boltzmann-weight plane) is studied. What is the ORDER of the phase transitions for the Z(4) lattice gauge model in the regime J2>=0? Answer in JSON with field "transition_order" using one of: "first_order", "continuous", "bkt".

metadata.system: Extended Z(4) lattice gauge model in 4D with two-parameter plaquette action, J2>=0 regime
metadata.source_excerpt: Monte Carlo data indicate that all transitions for J2>=0 (i.e., for models in the region above the Wilson-action curve X2=X1^2 in the (X1,X2) plane) are first order... latent-heat behavior
metadata.difficulty_note: The first-order character weakens as one moves from the Potts point toward the bifurcation; below the Wilson curve (J2<0) the picture changes and a massless/continuous phase may appear.

question: The 8-state Potts model on a two-dimensional square lattice is studied with a layered aperiodic modulation of nearest-neighbor couplings along one direction. The couplings take two values K0=K and K1=K*r (with coupling ratio r=5), arranged according to the paper-folding (PF) aperiodic sequence (generated by pair substitutions 11->1101, 10->1100, 01->1001, 00->1000 with last digit omitted), which has logarithmically divergent fluctuations (wandering exponent omega_PF=0). Without the aperiodic modulation, the 8-state Potts model has a FIRST-ORDER transition. Swendsen-Wang Monte Carlo finite-size scaling is used at the self-dual critical coupling. What is the ORDER of the phase transition of the 8-state Potts model with the paper-folding layered aperiodic sequence? Answer in JSON with field "transition_order" using one of: "first_order", "continuous", "bkt".

metadata.system: 8-state Potts model on 2D square lattice with paper-folding layered aperiodic coupling sequence
metadata.source_excerpt: For the 8-state Potts model with the paper-folding (PF) layered sequence... Monte Carlo finite-size scaling at the self-dual critical coupling... shows behavior consistent with a second-order (continuous) transition, indicating that paper-folding aperiodicity induces a change from first-order to second-order
metadata.difficulty_note: Aperiodic perturbation with wandering exponent omega=0 (logarithmically divergent fluctuations) is marginally relevant and induces an order change from first to second order; Thue-Morse (omega=-inf) is irrelevant and leaves first-order intact.

question: The biquadratic (quadrupolar) planar spin model is defined on a stacked triangular lattice (STL) with Hamiltonian H' = -J' * sum_{<i,j>} cos^2(phi_i - phi_j), where phi_i is the spin angle, with antiferroquadrupolar coupling J'<0 and positive interplane coupling. Monte Carlo finite-size scaling is used to measure the critical indices nu_q and gamma_q. What is the ORDER of the finite-temperature phase transition of the antiferroquadrupolar (J'<0) biquadratic exchange model on the stacked triangular lattice? Answer in JSON with field "transition_order" using one of: "first_order", "continuous", "bkt". Also include "universality_class" using one of: "3D XY", "chiral", "2D Ising", "3D Heisenberg", "other".

metadata.system: Antiferroquadrupolar biquadratic planar spin model on stacked triangular lattice, J'<0
metadata.source_excerpt: The antiferroquadrupolar biquadratic exchange model on the stacked triangular lattice (STL)... undergoes a second-order transition. Finite-size scaling analysis yields critical indices nu_q=0.520±0.003 and gamma_q=1.072±0.009... consistent with chiral universality values
metadata.difficulty_note: Nonchiral model (no ordered plaquette chirality) exhibits chiral universality class behavior; frustration/noncollinearity rather than plaquette chirality determines the universality class.

question: A mixed-spin Ising model is defined on a two-dimensional square lattice with Hamiltonian H = J1 * sum_{<i,j>} sigma_i * S_j + D * sum_{j in B} S_j^2 - J2 * sum_{<<i,j>> in B} S_i * S_j, where J1>0 is the antiferromagnetic nearest-neighbor coupling between A-sublattice spins sigma_i=±1 and B-sublattice spins S_j=0,±1, D is the single-site anisotropy on B, and J2 is the next-nearest-neighbor coupling among B spins. Monte Carlo simulations are used to study the phase transitions in the antiferromagnetic (AF), the 0I (half-B-spins in state S=0), and the B-AF phases. What is the ORDER and UNIVERSALITY CLASS of the finite-temperature phase transitions in the AF and 0I phases of this model? Answer in JSON with field "transition_order" using one of: "first_order", "continuous", "bkt", and "universality_class" using one of: "2D Ising", "3D Ising", "BKT", "other".

metadata.system: Mixed-spin Ising model (sigma=±1 and S=0,±1) on 2D square lattice with NNN B-B coupling
metadata.source_excerpt: For the AF (antiferromagnetic) phase, the 0I phase, and the B-AF phase, the finite-temperature transitions are in the two-dimensional Ising universality class (continuous second-order transitions with 2D Ising critical exponents)
metadata.difficulty_note: Model has compensation points (below Tc where sublattice magnetizations cancel) but the transition itself is standard 2D Ising universality class.

cold_solve.probe_model: claude-sonnet baseline (a cloud HPC platform sandbox for C1)

question: Ising spins s_i=±1 are located on the vertices of a triangulated sphere coupled to two-dimensional Euclidean quantum gravity via Regge calculus with the scale-invariant path-integral measure D mu(l) = prod_{<ij>} dl_{ij}/l_{ij} * F({l_{ij}}), where F ensures triangle inequalities. The total area is fixed at A = N2/2 (with N2 the number of triangles). High-statistics Monte Carlo finite-size scaling is performed on this dynamically triangulated sphere. The KPZ/DDK formalism predicts modified critical exponents (e.g., alpha/nu=-2/3) for Ising spins coupled to 2D gravity. What is the ORDER and UNIVERSALITY CLASS of the ferromagnetic-to-paramagnetic phase transition of the Ising model on this gravitating sphere (Regge calculus, scale-invariant measure)? Answer in JSON with field "transition_order" using one of: "first_order", "continuous", "bkt", and "universality_class" using one of: "2D Ising", "KPZ", "mean-field", "other".

metadata.system: Ising model on dynamically triangulated sphere coupled to 2D quantum gravity via Regge calculus (scale-invariant measure)
metadata.source_excerpt: Finite-size scaling analysis... yields critical-exponent estimates that are compatible with the flat-space (Onsager) universality class within the quoted errors: the weighted average for the inverse correlation-length exponent is 1/nu = 1.08(5)...
metadata.difficulty_note: KPZ gravity-modified exponents (predicted by matrix models/dynamical triangulations) are excluded; Regge calculus with scale-invariant measure gives flat-space Ising universality, contrary to KPZ expectations.

question: The t-V model of spinless fermions on a two-dimensional Lieb lattice is defined by nearest-neighbor hopping t and repulsive interaction V on a three-sublattice lattice (sublattices A, B, C per unit cell) at half-filling. Below a critical temperature T_c, the system spontaneously breaks particle-hole symmetry and develops a charge density wave. Quantum Monte Carlo simulations (auxiliary-field QMC) with finite-size scaling are performed for system sizes L=4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 15 and interaction strengths V/t = 0.75, 1, 1.5, 2, 3. What is the ORDER and UNIVERSALITY CLASS of the charge-density-wave phase transition of the t-V model on the Lieb lattice? Answer in JSON with field "transition_order" using one of: "first_order", "continuous", "bkt", and "universality_class" using one of: "2D Ising", "3D Ising", "BKT", "mean-field", "other".

metadata.system: t-V model (spinless fermions with NN hopping t and repulsion V) on 2D Lieb lattice at half-filling
metadata.source_excerpt: A finite-size scaling analysis of the squared order parameter m^2... shows excellent data collapse when the critical exponents are fixed to the two-dimensional Ising universality class, confirming a continuous transition in the 2D Ising class
metadata.difficulty_note: CT-INT QMC has heavy-tailed distributions making variance ill-defined; AF-QMC is more reliable for this system. Weak-coupling Tc ~ V^2 (vs mean-field V), strong-coupling approaches classical Ising limit.

question: The frozen-modulus SU(2)-Higgs model on a four-dimensional lattice at gauge coupling beta=2.3 is defined by S_inf = sum_x (1/4) * kappa_1 * Tr[Phi_dag(x) U_mu(x) Phi(x+mu)] + (beta/4) * sum_P Tr U_P, where Phi_dag Phi=1 (fixed modulus). An extended action adds a next-to-nearest-neighbor gauge-Higgs hopping term with coupling kappa_2. Monte Carlo simulations on L^4 lattices (L up to 24) measure energy histograms, latent heats, and Binder cumulant minima for various kappa_1 values along the Confinement-Higgs phase boundary. What is the ORDER of the Confinement-Higgs phase transition of the frozen-modulus SU(2)-Higgs model at kappa_2=0 (standard action)? Answer in JSON with field "transition_order" using one of: "first_order", "continuous", "bkt".

metadata.system: Frozen-modulus SU(2)-Higgs model on 4D L^4 lattice at beta=2.3, Confinement-Higgs phase boundary
metadata.source_excerpt: The combined evidence from the extended-parameter study (a connected line of first-order transitions for kappa_2>0 that weakens continuously as kappa_2->0), the measured nonzero latent heats along that line, and the finite-size specific-heat and Binder cumulant behavior, indicates that the Confinement-Higgs transition for the standard action is consistent with a first-order transition, albeit extremely weak and transient on accessible lattices
metadata.difficulty_note: Extremely weak first-order at kappa_2=0; two-peak distributions not resolved up to L=24 for standard action; first-order inferred from connected kappa_2>0 line and asymptotic Binder cumulant analysis.
